text.title: Jobs To Be Done (Researcher) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Jobs To Be Done (Researcher) application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Interviews target switchers who moved within the last 90 days. Older switchers' memory of forces is degraded.
rule.rationale: Episodic memory of the switching moment degrades quickly; recent switchers reconstruct forces with higher fidelity.

rule.statement: Every transcript tags Push / Pull / Habit / Fear forces. Missing any force category triggers an interview re-run probe.
rule.rationale: All four forces operate during a switch; missing one signals an interview gap.

rule.statement: Each interview anchors on a single observable switch event (sign-up, cancel, tool change). Generic 'I started using X' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without observable events the interviewee reconstructs a plausible story, not the switch.

rule.statement: Forces aggregate via the rubric (strength 1–5 × evidence count). Aggregated forces with strength <3 don't make the hire/fire criteria.
rule.rationale: Without rubric the strongest narrative wins, not the strongest signal.

rule.statement: Output ends with hire/fire criteria: under what conditions a customer hires this product, under what conditions they fire it.
rule.rationale: Without hire/fire criteria the JTBD output is theory, not product input.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
